markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and parent company Take-Two have locked in a November 19, 2026 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI after two prior delays from the original 2025 window, with the timeline reaffirmed in recent earnings guidance projecting strong fiscal 2027 bookings. This extended runway through the remainder of 2026 heightens trader focus on whether major entertainment milestones—such as high-profile album drops from artists like Rihanna or Playboi Carti, major AI advancements like GPT-6, or political shifts—will occur first. The gaming giant’s history of polish-driven postponements keeps the door open for last-minute changes, while fan anticipation and streaming metrics around related content continue to build cultural momentum ahead of the launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial on May 15, 2026—after a hung jury on the remaining charge—has anchored trader consensus around “No Prison Time” at 75.3% implied probability. Prosecutors now confront repeated deadlocks on the same allegations following the 2020 conviction’s overturn and a 2025 partial verdict, diminishing appetite for a fourth trial. Weinstein, already serving a consecutive 16-year California sentence for prior sexual assault convictions, faces mounting procedural barriers, health concerns at age 74, and potential concurrent plea resolutions that would add no further time. These factors have compressed odds on all additional-sentence brackets below 10%, reflecting the market’s assessment of entrenched legal and practical headwinds.

markets.description:

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights remain neck-and-neck in the 2026 Stanley Cup futures market due to their parallel paths through the conference finals. Carolina posted the league's top regular-season record and holds a series lead over Montreal, reflecting strong depth and home-ice consistency that traders price at 47.5 percent. Vegas, meanwhile, surged after sweeping the top-seeded Avalanche under new coaching, positioning the Pacific Division leaders at 44.9 percent while they await the Eastern winner. The Montreal Canadiens trail at 7.4 percent after an unexpected run but face elimination risk in a best-of-seven matchup against the more experienced Hurricanes. These recent playoff results and series dynamics keep the top two outcomes tightly bunched, with each team's advancement hinging on the next few games.

The Montreal Canadiens trail at 7.4 percent after an unexpected run but face elimination risk in a best-of-seven matchup against the more experienced Hurricanes. These recent playoff results and series dynamics keep the top two outcomes tightly bunched, with each team's advancement hinging on the next few games.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's removal of presidential term limits in 2018 and his unanimous third-term election as CCP general secretary in 2022 and state president in 2023 entrenched his authority without fixed succession timelines. Recent diplomatic activity, including May 2026 summits with U.S. President Trump and Russian President Putin plus ongoing foreign engagements, underscores his active leadership role with no public signals of transition. The CCP's emphasis on centralized control and Xi Jinping Thought further stabilizes his position through 2026. Trader consensus at 93% for "No" aligns with this structural continuity and absence of near-term catalysts for change.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 24% in this early 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, reflecting his visibility as California governor, national fundraising network, and positioning through book tours and criticism of the Trump administration. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 10% on progressive support, while Kamala Harris sits at 8% amid ongoing book-tour outreach and name recognition from the prior cycle. Recent May 2026 polls show Harris and Newsom trading leads depending on the survey, underscoring how the wide-open field after 2024 hinges on name identification, primary-state travel, and appeal across ideological wings. Key differentiators include executive records versus legislative or national profiles, with consolidation likely driven by early endorsements, debate performances, and shifts in voter coalitions before formal announcements.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 24% in this early 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, reflecting his visibility as California governor, national fundraising network, and positioning through book tours and criticism of the Trump administration. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 10% on progressive support, while Kamala Harris sits at 8% amid ongoing book-tour outreach and name recognition from the prior cycle. Recent May 2026 polls show Harris and Newsom trading leads depending on the survey, underscoring how the wide-open field after 2024 hinges on name identification, primary-state travel, and appeal across ideological wings. Key differentiators include executive records versus legislative or national profiles, with consolidation likely driven by early endorsements, debate performances, and shifts in voter coalitions before formal announcements.
